content: Maximum number of external links created

comment.text: The "Maximum number of external links created" translation key is used to display a tooltip or text label in the UI, indicating the maximum limit of external links allowed. It appears on an icon button and is part of the info panel on the home page, providing context for users when viewing certain types of content.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Arabic translation is a complete sentence ('Maximum number of external links has been created') instead of a noun phrase/label ('Maximum number of external links created'). This changes the meaning from a limit/count description to a status notification.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: الحد الأقصى للروابط الخارجية التي تم إنشاؤها

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Greek translation is a complete sentence in the past tense ('Maximum number of external links was created'), whereas the English source is a noun phrase describing a limit or a metric ('Maximum number of external links created'). In a UI context (tooltip/label), the Greek version sounds like a status notification rather than a descriptive label for a limit.

The Polish translation 'Utworzono maksymalną liczbę linków zewnętrznych' translates to 'The maximum number of external links has been created' (a statement of fact/completion), whereas the English text 'Maximum number of external links created' in this UI context refers to a limit or a count of how many have been created (a label/metric).

The English text is a noun phrase describing a limit ('Maximum number of external links created'), whereas the Romanian translation is a complete sentence stating that the limit has already been exceeded ('The maximum allowed number of external links has been exceeded').
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Numărul maxim de link-uri externe create

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The word order in Serbian is grammatically incorrect for a label/tooltip. 'креирано' (created) is in the neuter singular form, but it should agree with 'број' (number), which is masculine, or the sentence structure should be adjusted to sound natural in Serbian.
The translation uses a passive participle 'kreirano' (created) which is grammatically incorrect in this context. It should be 'kreiranih' to correctly modify 'broj' (number) or 'linkova' (links).

The Turkish translation uses a passive verb form ('oluşturuldu' - 'were created') which implies a count of links already made, whereas the English text 'Maximum number of external links created' in this UI context refers to the limit/capacity (the maximum number allowed to be created).
